title: What does this mean - "Sending of the message failed. The message was not sent due to exceeding the allowed number of recipients. The server responded: Policy-SC01 You’ve reached your sending limit. Don’t worry, you can send again soon.."
creator: pldhort
content: I regularly get the following message when attempting to send an email :- "Sending of the message failed. The message was not sent due to exceeding the allowed number of recipients. The server responded: Policy-SC01 You’ve reached your sending limit. Don’t worry, you can send again soon.." This message implies that I am attempting to send a message with multiple recipients, BUT it only happens when I am sending to only ONE recipient. Why is this, and how do I eliminate this very annoying restriction. I currently cannot send an email because this problem is preventing me from doing so. Thunderbird has been a useful email client for many years, but these frustrations are not helping me to remain with Firebird. Regards, PD
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/148.0.0.0 Safari/537.36 Edg/148.0.0.0;tb_version:151.0;os:Windows 11

title: Thunderbird Login Outlook.com Mailbox Port Clash Bug
creator: akarinliu
content:
我最近遇到了无法发送邮件的问题，我尝试删除账户重新登录，但是遇到了一个问题：获取登录信息默认使用本地电脑的 443 端口，但是与 Steamcommunity 302 和 Laravel Herd 等涉及使用 https 端口的软件冲突，希望能够改为随机端口以解决该问题。

I recently ran into an issue where I tried to delete my account and re-login, but I had a problem: getting login information was used by port 443 of the local computer by default, but with Steamcommunity 302 and Laravel Herald and other software conflicts involving the use of https port, hoping to change to a random port to resolve the issue.

content:
I have two email accounts (one is imap, one is pop3) in my older version of Thunderbird. They have both worked normally until last week. title: Filter running twice?
creator: arw2104
content:
I get several daily email newsletters. I've set up a separate filter for each of the senders to move all these incoming newsletters to a local folder. I've attached a screenshot of one of the filters, but it's pretty straightforward: Move the email to the local folder, then stop filter execution.

Sometimes, but not always, it seems to run twice, and puts two copies of the newsletter into the local folder.

Note that the filter for "Morning Brew" and "Axios" have the stop execution step, while the filter for "1440" does not. The same behavior seems to happen (again, occasionally) for each filter, with or without the "stop execution".

I see another user having a similar issue here: Filter Issue

Any suggestions?

title: Why is Thunderbird suddenly not receiving messages (I already checked my password settings)?
creator: mindimindy
content: I've been using Thunderbird for years but suddenly as of yesterday it's stopped receiving messages. First it told me my password was incorrect. Ever since then when I try to click "Get Messages," it simply has no response at all. Nothing happens. I went into my Thunderbird password settings, and upon seeing that the incoming-message password had for some reason reverted to an old, obsolete password, I corrected it and saved the correct one. I've checked several times and the passwords are correctly saved now. But this did not fix the problem. When I click "Get Messages," nothing happens. My Thunderbird is connected to my Earthlink account, which is working fine. When I go into Earthlink, I am able to send and receive messages as normal. So the problem is not with Earthlink but with Thunderbird.
